title: hook_after implies hook_after_any
manager: Registering an "after" hook on a specific transition should also fire the "after any" observers, but right now the general subscription is silently skipped when a specific one exists. Code that watches every transition — a logger, a tracer — quietly develops holes exactly where a named handler happens to be attached, which is maddening to debug because the missing events look random. Fix so the specific hook and the any-hook both fire.
